title: From Control To Surrender
study: A Study In The Life Of King David
scriptureForWeek: This week covers the events of the Ark of the Covenant being brought into Jerusalem. We will look at 2 Samuel 2 and 1 Chronicles 15.
weekTheme: Boys worry, Men worship God will reward our devotion What’s the first that comes to mind when you think of worship? Is it choirs singing in high-pitched voices in white gowns? Is it a Christian rock concert with low lights and people lifting their hands in the air? Many guys your age tend to think of worship in terms of the style or the response, but that can miss the point. Worship is more than just singing or tradition. Worship is the wholehearted response a man gives to what he values most. Everyone worships. Guys your age might not know they are worshippers, but when you understand what it really means, you will see that everyone worships something. Some guys worship video games, some guys worship girls, some guys worship sports. Michael Morrison explains this further: The English word "worship" comes from two Old English words: weorth, which means "worth," and scipe or ship, which means something like shape or "quality." We can see the Old English word -ship in modern words like friendship and sportsmanship – that’s the quality of being a friend, or the quality of being a good sport. So worth-ship is the quality of having worth or of being worthy. When we worship, we are saying that God has worth, that he is worthy. Worship means to declare worth, to attribute worth. Or to put it in biblical terms, we praise God. We speak, or sing, about how good and powerful God is. To be honest, you may think the idea of worshipping God is boring. He can seem distant or far away, and church can be filled with stuff that can be hard to understand. But when you realize what God is actually like, you are going to respond like a fan watching their favorite team score a goal or a touchdown. Sometimes when you are getting to know God and learning

days.title: Reverence And Reputation
days.scripture: 2 SAMUEL 6:1-11 Then David again gathered all the elite troops in Israel, 30,000 in all. He led them to Baalah of Judah to bring back the Ark of God, which bears the name of the LORD of Heaven’s Armies, who is enthroned between the cherubim. They placed the Ark of God on a new cart and brought it from Abinadab’s house, which was on a hill. Uzzah and Ahio, Abinadab’s sons, were guiding the cart that carried the Ark of God. Ahio walked in front of the Ark. David and all the people of Israel were celebrating before the LORD, singing songs and playing all kinds of musical instruments—lyres, harps, tambourines, castanets, and cymbals. But when they arrived at the threshing floor of Nacon, the oxen stumbled, and Uzzah reached out his hand and steadied the Ark of God. Then the LORD’s anger was aroused against Uzzah, and God struck him dead because of this. So Uzzah died right there beside the Ark of God. David was angry because the LORD’s anger had burst out against Uzzah. He named that place Perez-uzzah (which means “to burst out against Uzzah”), as it is still called today. David was now afraid of the LORD, and he asked, “How can I ever bring the Ark of the LORD back into my care?” 10 So David decided not to move the Ark of the LORD into the City of David. Instead, he took it to the house of Obed-edom of Gath. 11 The Ark of the LORD remained there in Obed-edom’s house for three months, and the LORD blessed Obed-edom and his entire household.
days.reflection: How can you tell if God is with you? Is it a feeling you feel? Is it knowing what the bible says? It is some belief you hold because your parents tell you to? Is it something you go along with so you don’t get in trouble? In the Old Testament, in the time of King David, God's presence was known by the Ark of the Covenant. The Ark of the Covenant was “a wooden chest, covered in pure gold, with an elaborately designed lid called the mercy seat. The Ark contained the two stone tablets of the Ten Commandments, and Aaron's rod and a pot of manna.” Now these details seem weird, but they were important to the Israelites’ history and worship. The ark was the symbol of God’s presence among his people. If the ark was with you, God was with you. But the Philistines had captured the ark a long while back, and David wanted it back. So he went to get it and was excited to bring the sign of God's presence back to Jerusalem so the people could feel safe and secure. David was so excited about getting the ark back that he took 30,000 of his elite soldiers and a massive group of musicians. It was a huge party, until… One of David’s men, Uzzah, reached out to steady the ark when it hit a bump in the road, and then, bang - God struck him dead. Dang. That seems pretty heavy, doesn’t it? The Ark was about to fall to the ground and he reaches out to help and God kills him. What is going on here? The simple answer is this: in all of his excitement, David had failed to worship God properly. He carried the Ark in a way it wasn’t supposed to be, and he was a little too familiar with God's presence and power. So David got angry and afraid of God and didn’t bring it back to Jerusalem. He was confused and hurt and didn’t know what to do. You live in an age where people have no respect for God. They use the name of Jesus as a swear word. People mock religion and faith, and any sense of gratitude and fear of God is basically gone. But this doesn’t mean that God has changed. He is still the creator of heaven and earth, and we should be humble and grateful before him. When Jesus taught his disciples to pray, the first thing he said was, “Our Father in heaven, hallowed be your name.” Real men show God reverence and respect. If you went to the White House and met the President, you would dress your best, sharpen your manners, and be on your best behavior. How much more powerful and important is God than a man? Think about that next time you think of worship. David learned to honor God the hard way, and you would be wise to have that kind of attitude in your heart. God is love, but God is mighty. Holding that in tension will serve you well in the coming years.
questions: Do you think it was fair for Uzzah to die like this? Why or why not?
Have you ever had a moment when you felt God's presence in worship? If so, what was it like?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share some ways they learned to treat God with reverence and respect. Why do they think it’s important that you learn this at this stage of your life?

thoughts.verse: Since we are receiving a Kingdom that is unshakable, let us be thankful and please God by worshiping him with holy fear and awe. For our God is a devouring fire.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God for a fresh sense of reverence and awe at who he is.

days.scripture: 1 CHRONICLES 15:1-3, 11-14 David now built several buildings for himself in the City of David. He also prepared a place for the Ark of God and set up a special tent for it. Then he commanded, “No one except the Levites may carry the Ark of God. The LORD has chosen them to carry the Ark of the LORD and to serve him forever.” Then David summoned all Israel to Jerusalem to bring the Ark of the LORD to the place he had prepared for it…. Then David summoned the priests, Zadok and Abiathar, and these Levite leaders: Uriel, Asaiah, Joel, Shemaiah, Eliel, and Amminadab. He said to them, “You are the leaders of the Levite families. You must purify yourselves and all your fellow Levites, so you can bring the Ark of the LORD, the God of Israel, to the place I have prepared for it. Because you Levites did not carry the Ark the first time, the anger of the LORD our God burst out against us. We failed to ask God how to move it properly.” So the priests and the Levites purified themselves in order to bring the Ark of the LORD, the God of Israel, to Jerusalem.
days.reflection: Because you Levites did not carry the Ark the first time, the anger of the LORD our God burst out against us. We failed to ask God how to move it properly. One of the key lessons you will learn as you grow into a man is respect. Respecting others, respecting yourself, respecting authority. But the most important thing to learn is respecting God. God is love, and God is kind, but God is also holy. He is powerful and mighty, and he has a reason for the things that he asks. You don’t just relate to God on your terms; you relate to God on his terms. Yesterday you saw David got a little too familiar with God and failed to respect God's commands. He was so excited about the presence of God being restored through the Ark that he forgot to ask God how to bring it in. This made David afraid, and for a while, he was too scared to approach the presence of God. But David doesn’t give up on his relationship with God; he revisits the foundation of it. He studies God's word to see where things went wrong. He realized he had been far too casual with God and had not shown him the reverence he deserved. You see, God had given very clear instructions of how his presence was to be treated. He was very clear about how the Ark was to be carried. The first time David went for the Ark, he went with warriors. But not this time. God had commanded the Levites to carry the Ark on their shoulders; it wasn’t meant to be treated as a common thing and just dumped on a cart. And David had the worshippers lead the procession, not the warriors. God wanted his people to know he wasn’t just a symbol for success in battle or a good luck charm so that things would go their way. God was someone worthy of honor, reverence, and respect. He was to be celebrated for who he was. David learned that God had to be honored, not just used. Now you may be thinking what does all this stuff from the Old Testament have to do with you today? No one is carrying an Ark into church, and God isn’t striking people down for doing church wrong. It is true that we are in a different time of history and a different kind of relationship with God because of what Jesus has done for us. But we must still show God reverence today. Even in the New Testament God will still discipline us when we disobey him. Discipline is different from punishment, but it still hurts. In Acts 5, Ananias and Sapphira were struck down for lying to the Apostles about how much money they gave to the church. In 1 Corinthians 11, God disciplined the church for taking communion in a dishonoring way and excluding other people. And in Revelation 2, God warns a local church in Thyatira that he will discipline them with suffering and death if they don’t repent for despising him and worshipping idols with rebellious hearts. David learned to respect God and respond properly to his love, and as a young man, it’s important that you do too. Don’t fall into the trap so many guys do at your age, thinking that you can do whatever you want without any sort of consequences. Your relationship with God is so important; give God the respect he deserves and he will give you blessings and favor that you don’t deserve. You are on track; keep taking things seriously like you are and it will be worth it in the end.
questions: Why is it wrong to just approach God however we want? Why do you think approaching God the right way matters to him?
Why do you think guys your age tend to lack respect for God? What’s happening in the world around us that makes this acceptable?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share how they really learned to worship God with reverence and awe. What happened in their heart to create this sense of respect for God?

thoughts.verse: Fear of the LORD is the foundation of true wisdom. All who obey his commandments will grow in wisdom. Psalms 111:10 Read through his verse slowly a second time. Is there something that stands out to you in it? Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean? Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to honor God in your heart. Position yourself before him with humility and gratitude. Thank him for his kindness and love.

days.title: Obedience
days.scripture: 2 SAMUEL 6:12-15 Then King David was told, “The LORD has blessed Obed- edom’s household and everything he has because of the Ark of God.” So David went there and brought the Ark of God from the house of Obed-edom to the City of David with a great celebration. After the men who were carrying the Ark of the LORD had gone six steps, David sacrificed a bull and a fattened calf. And David danced before the LORD with all his might, wearing a priestly garment. So David and all the people of Israel brought up the Ark of the LORD with shouts of joy and the blowing of rams’ horns.
days.reflection: How do you know if you really love God? Have you ever thought about that? Is love for God some kind of weird religious feeling? Is love for God trying to be good? Is love for God going on some big mission to try and save the world? The good news is that Jesus explicitly shares how we can really love God. In John 14:21, he says, “Those who accept my commandments and obey them are the ones who love me. And because they love me, my Father will love them. And I will love them and reveal myself to each of them.” Love for God is found in doing what God wants and doing it God's way. It’s surrendering your will to his and learning to trust him. Now obedience and love and commands may not sound like much fun for a guy your age, but God always does asks for this with your good in mind. He is not trying to oppress you; he is trying to protect you. He is not trying to frustrate you; he is trying to help you flourish. David realized God had commanded his people (Numbers 7:9) to carry the Ark on the shoulders of men because it was sacred. And David wanted to make sure that he honored God by having the priests do that. Then David offered a sacrifice every 6 steps, to let God know how serious he was about honoring and obeying him. Bible scholars tell us that based on the distance from Obed Edom's house to Jerusalem, it would have taken around 3500 sacrifices before the Ark made it to Jerusalem. How crazy is that? Talk about a change of heart. David has gone from casual obedience to serious commitment. Make sure you notice the result of David’s obedience. Is he beaten down? Is he sad that God has asked so much of him? No way! And David danced before the LORD with all his might, wearing a priestly garment. So David and all the people of Israel brought up the Ark of the LORD with shouts of joy and the blowing of rams’ horns. David is losing his mind. David is loving it. He is leaping and dancing with everything he has. Imagine a crowd of people after their favorite team wins the super bowl. People screaming, crying, jumping up and down, hugging each other. For David, that’s the kind of joy that obedience to God leads to. This wasn’t a beatdown; this was a party. When you decide to be a man who obeys God, there will be times that it's hard and painful. There will also be times of incredible joy. But remember this, doing things God's way leads to God's joy. And that joy is better than anything this world can offer. Better than having your friends like you, better than getting into an amazing college, better than your parents getting you a car, better than dating an amazing girl. David would go on to say in Psalm 63:3-5: Your unfailing love is better than life itself; how I praise you! I will praise you as long as I live, lifting up my hands to you in prayer. You satisfy me more than the richest feast. I will praise you with songs of joy. Joy! Satisfaction! Love! This is the life of faith. Learn to choose it now and you will be amazed how much more God will give you down the road.
questions: Have you ever had a moment when you felt the choice to obey or disobey God? What was it, and what did you choose? How did it make you feel afterward?
What are one or two things you could do to help other guys your age learn that obeying God leads to joy and not a beatdown?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share a time they obeyed God and experience his joy. What did they do, and how did that joy show up?

thoughts.verse: We know we love God’s children if we love God and obey his commandments. Loving God means keeping his commandments, and his commandments are not burdensome. 1 John 5:2-3 Read through his verse slowly a second time. Is there something that stands out to you in it? Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean? Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to give you a vision of becoming a young man who lives according to God's word and seeks to obey his commandments. Ask him for help in any areas where you feel like you are struggling to obey him.

days.scripture: 2 SAMUEL 6:12-15, 20-23 AND 1 CHRONICLES 15:16-27-29 Then King David was told, “The LORD has blessed Obed- edom’s household and everything he has because of the Ark of God.” So David went there and brought the Ark of God from the house of Obed-edom to the City of David with a great celebration. After the men who were carrying the Ark of the LORD had gone six steps, David sacrificed a bull and a fattened calf. And David danced before the LORD with all his might, wearing a priestly garment. So David and all the people of Israel brought up the Ark of the LORD with shouts of joy and the blowing of rams’ horns. David told the leaders of the Levites to appoint their fellow Levites as musicians to make a joyful sound with musical instruments: lyres, harps and cymbals… Now David was clothed in a robe of fine linen, as were all the Levites who were carrying the ark, and as were the musicians, and Kenaniah, who was in charge of the singing of the choirs. David also wore a linen ephod. So all Israel brought up the ark of the covenant of the LORD with shouts, with the sounding of rams’ horns and trumpets, and of cymbals, and the playing of lyres and harps. As the ark of the covenant of the LORD was entering the City of David, Michal daughter of Saul watched from a window. And when she saw King David dancing and celebrating, she despised him in her heart. When David returned home to bless his household, Michal daughter of Saul came out to meet him and said, “How the king of Israel has distinguished himself today, going around half-naked in full view of the slave girls of his servants as any vulgar fellow would!” David said to Michal, “It was before the LORD, who chose me rather than your father or anyone from his house when he appointed me ruler over the LORD’s people Israel—I will celebrate before the LORD. I will become even more undignified than this, and I will be humiliated in my own eyes. But by these slave girls you spoke of, I will be held in honor.” And Michal daughter of Saul had no children to the day of her death.
days.reflection: Do you care what people think of you? Do you ever change your behavior to make people like you or to fit in? Most people do because they want to be liked by others. It takes a ton of confidence to just be yourself and live free from the opinions of others. Think about dancing in public. Do you like dancing in front of others at weddings or parties, or do you normally just sit on the side and watch others because you feel awkward? So often guys your age can be so worried about what others think of them that they never feel free to express what really matters to them. King David didn’t seem to care what others thought. In today’s passage, he is living his absolute best life. The Ark of the Covenant is coming into Jerusalem and it’s a citywide party. There are musicians, singers, instruments, and dancers. And leading them out the front is David. Now in those days, kings were supposed to be strong, serious, and regal. They ruled with power and respect. They were not supposed to be dancing down the road at huge street fairs. But David doesn’t seem to care. And David danced before the LORD with all his might, wearing a priestly garment. David isn’t dressed like a king; David is dressed for worship. He has the priest's clothes on and he is getting his worship on. You see, there are a couple of small words in this passage that change everything for David. They explain his behavior. Take another look and see if you can spot them. That’s right, he danced BEFORE THE LORD. David wasn’t performing, pretending, or trying to fit in. He was celebrating before God and doing it for him. He didn’t care what people thought because he wasn’t doing it for them. He wanted to worship with all his might and he did. He was a man living from a full heart. But not everyone appreciated this. When he got home, his wife was mad. She felt ashamed that he wasn’t acting properly, mixing with the commoners and getting too close to some of the women at the celebration. She told him he was acting like a “vulgar fellow” and no one is cool with that. David responded, "I will become even more undignified than this, and I will be humiliated in my own eyes. But by these slave girls you spoke of, I will be held in honor.” If you are going to be serious about living for God, people will criticize and judge you. Try not to worry about that. You will never be able to keep people happy. If you do what they want to be liked, you will have to keep doing it to stay friends, and then you are just a puppet of others’ opinions. Be your own man before God. Live for him with joy and freedom. Surrender your reputation to him, and he will take care of it over the long haul. After all, who do you think was having more fun that day? David or his wife? You are going to learn that there is wild joy in the presence of God. Don’t let others’ opinions of you rob you of that.
questions: Can you think of a time when you acted how you really felt without caring what anyone thought at all? Maybe you were mad, sad, or just super happy, but you just let your true feelings out. What was that time, and what did you do?
Based on your observations, what are a couple of reasons why guys your age care so much about other people’s opinions of them?

Ask the person you are doing this study with to share a time they got lost or caught up in worship without caring what anyone else thought. Where were they, what happened, and how did it make them feel?

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. Whatever you do, work at it with all your heart, as working for the Lord, not for human masters, since you know that you will receive an inheritance from the Lord as a reward. It is the Lord Christ you are serving.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in any of them?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to free you from caring about what people think of you in any way that has become unhealthy. Ask him for boldness and courage to live for him.

days.title: Ignoring The Haters
days.scripture: 1 CHRONICLES 15:16-25-29 So David and the elders of Israel and the commanders of units of a thousand went to bring up the ark of the covenant of the LORD from the house of Obed-Edom, with rejoicing. Because God had helped the Levites who were carrying the ark of the covenant of the LORD, seven bulls and seven rams were sacrificed. Now David was clothed in a robe of fine linen, as were all the Levites who were carrying the ark, and as were the musicians, and Kenaniah, who was in charge of the singing of the choirs. David also wore a linen ephod. So all Israel brought up the ark of the covenant of the LORD with shouts, with the sounding of rams’ horns and trumpets, and of cymbals, and the playing of lyres and harps. As the ark of the covenant of the LORD was entering the City of David, Michal daughter of Saul watched from a window. And when she saw King David dancing and celebrating, she despised him in her heart.
days.reflection: There is an old saying that goes like this: “There is only one way to avoid criticism: do nothing, say nothing, and be nothing.” You live in a time of history where you will be criticized for anything and everything. People will criticize you if you do something or if don’t do anything. People will criticize you if you stand up or if you sit down. People will criticize you if you speak up or say nothing. Criticism sucks. And over the course of your life, you are going to get a lot more of it. The teenage years can be really painful years where it feels like there is so much extra criticism too. But don’t let it paralyze you. You can entrust your reputation to God and surrender it to him. Trying to control all the criticism coming against you is a waste of time. So, are you going to let criticism make you do nothing, say nothing, and be nothing? NO! The best way to deal with criticism is not to ignore it. The best way to deal with it is to put it in context. There is always a reason people criticize others. You may ask some questions like…. Is this person criticizing me because they are jealous? Is this person criticizing me to make themselves look good? Is this person criticizing me because they are insecure? Is this person criticizing me because they have a mean spirit? Is this person criticizing me because I deserve it? Learning to deal with criticism can be tricky and frustrating. But you have to learn to do what you know is right in spite of it, and let God deal with the rest. This is what King David did. When his wife despised him in her heart for his passionate worship, he didn’t stop praising God. He didn’t let her criticism and judgment hold him back or shut him down. He knew that God wanted passionate worship and that passion always provokes a reaction from those who are lukewarm. It says in this passage that God was helping bring the Ark into the city; yet, David’s wife was criticizing the very thing that God was doing. You will learn as you get older people often criticize the very things they should be praising. Jesus experienced this too. Jesus came into the world to save the world from its sins. He came to bring life, tell us the truth, show us God's love, and bring God's kingdom; yet, people rejected him and hated him for doing the very thing they needed. But Jesus didn’t let this stop him from his mission. He loved, taught, healed, prayed, and trained his followers. Then he died for the sins of the very people who criticized him to give them life. Jesus overcame criticism by doing what God said. He left his reputation up to the Father and got on with the work. If David and Jesus faced criticism, you will too. But learn to respond like they did: by getting on with the work at hand and doing what you know is right. God will sort it out in the end. Surrender to him and serve with all your heart. There is an amazing freedom in that - one that guys your age rarely get to enjoy.
questions: What are some of the hardest criticisms you have faced? Who was it from and how did it make you feel?
What sort of areas do you see your friends criticize others in? What do you tend to criticize others for?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share how they learned to handle criticism as a man. Where do they still struggle? How do they work through it?

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. Fearing people is a dangerous trap, but trusting the LORD means safety.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in any of them?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to pray that God will give you the courage to handle any criticism you may be facing right now.

extras: WEEK 7 MANSCHOOL UGLY CARDIGAN BOWLING MAN SCHOOL REMINDER The recipe for fun is pretty simple when you’re raising boys: add to any activity an element of danger, stir in a little exploration, add a dash of destruction, and you’ve got yourself a winner. - John Eldredge OPTION 1. ACTIVITY: UGLY CARDIGAN BOWLING Go to a thrift shop and find the ugliest cardigan possible with your son. Then, go bowling wearing the sweaters together. Ignore the folks giving you weird looks and have a killer time together. OPTION 2. WATCH RAIDERS OF THE LOST ARK If you didn’t watch this one together yet, this is the time. An absolute classic. It follows a failed attempt to find the lost ark of the covenant, with some good warnings about trying to use God's power for yourself. Rare to get such a direct biblical reference these days ☺ https://youtu.be/0xQSIdSRlAk OPTION 3. DINNER AND DISCUSSION Take your son out to one of his favorite restaurants or for desserts and talk through what it means to handle criticism and rejection. Talk through the importance of caring about God's opinion more than the opinion of others. This could be a week where he really opens up and shares who his friends are, whose opinions matter most to him, how he fits in socially, and how he thinks about his faith. This could give you a clear picture of the relational and power dynamics of his life at this point. And whatever he shares - DON’T CRITICIZE. Just listen ☺
FIELD NOTES PRAYER FOR MY SON
